task: Implemented a two-voice sonic logo for session-done announcements by refactoring the voice module to support per-voice control, adding a `speak_signature()` function that plays a branded 'Done.' prefix in one voice followed by contextual detail in a second voice, and integrated it into the stop hook with randomized generic phrases for no-goal sessions.
discussion_summary: This session extended the voice announcement system beyond single-voice TTS to support askr's distinctive two-tone notification pattern. The refactor extracted shared preconditions (`_say_preconditions()`) to avoid duplication, added a `voice=` parameter to `speak()` for single-voice calls, and introduced `speak_signature(prefix, body, prefix_voice, body_voice)` for the branded done ping. The stop hook was updated to call `speak_signature("Done.", body, load_voice_prefix(), load_voice_body())` where body is either the first completed goal or a randomly-selected generic phrase when no goals completed but the turn ran long enough. All 161 tests pass (156 prior + 5 new covering the two-voice signature). The implementation was validated live through the actual code path using the project's venv Python interpreter.

accomplishments.what: Identified root cause of voice announcement timing bug in _turn_elapsed_seconds()
Fixed entry filter in askr/hooks/stop.py to skip tool_result-shaped user lines
Added regression test to test_voice.py covering real transcript shape
Verified all 156 tests pass after fix
Discovered PATH clobbering side effect in askr.session.lifecycle module-level import
Built end-to-end test harness with zsh shim to inject fake say command while preserving system binaries
Validated voice announcement gating across four scenarios: fast turn (silent), slow turn (announces), autonomous relaunch (early-return), multi-turn timing
Refactored voice.py to extract shared preconditions into _say_preconditions()
Added voice= parameter to speak() function for per-voice control
Implemented speak_signature(prefix, body, prefix_voice, body_voice) for two-voice sonic logo
Added _GENERIC_DONE_PHRASES rotation for no-goal session-done announcements
Updated _speak_session_done() to use speak_signature() with voice config loaders
Added 5 new test cases to test_voice.py covering speak_signature and voice parameter
Verified all 161 tests pass (156 prior + 5 new)
Validated live voice announcement through actual code path using venv Python

next_actions.action: Commit all five uncommitted files (askr/clients/voice.py, askr/hooks/stop.py, askr/state/config.py, tests/test_voice.py, askr_state/implementation_bippin.jsonl) to persist the two-voice sonic logo implementation
next_actions.why: Core feature implementation is complete and tested; changes must be committed to git to preserve work

next_actions.action: Verify that load_voice_prefix() and load_voice_body() config loaders exist and return valid macOS voice names (e.g. 'Good News', 'Zarvox'); add them to askr/state/config.py if missing
next_actions.why: The speak_signature() call in stop.py depends on these config loaders; they must be implemented for the feature to work in production

next_actions.action: Test the two-voice sonic logo in a real multi-turn session to confirm the 'Done.' prefix and detail play sequentially without overlap and sound distinct
next_actions.why: Live validation ensures the user experience matches the design intent of a recognizable branded notification

next_actions.action: Implement unit tests for voice announcement gating with tool_result entries (goal a8526cce) — add dedicated test cases to test_voice.py that explicitly cover scenarios where tool_results appear in the transcript
next_actions.why: Open goal from prior session; ensures regression coverage for the tool_result filtering logic

next_actions.action: Verify session-done announcement timing across multi-turn conversations (goal 93e0ada1) — run production-like multi-turn sessions and log announcement cadence to confirm no spam on long turns
next_actions.why: Open goal from prior session; validates that the per-turn gate works correctly in realistic multi-turn workflows

decisions.decision: Filter tool_result-shaped entries (those with 'tool_call_id' or 'tool_name' fields) when searching for the last real user message in _turn_elapsed_seconds()
decisions.reason: tool_results are logged under type='user' but represent system responses, not human input; filtering ensures the elapsed-time gate measures from the actual last user message

decisions.decision: Implement two-voice sonic logo for session-done announcements: a short branded 'Done.' prefix in one voice followed by contextual detail in a second voice
decisions.reason: Distinguishes askr's done notification from generic TTS alerts; the constant prefix is recognizable while the detail voice adds variety and prevents monotony

decisions.decision: Use randomized generic phrases (_GENERIC_DONE_PHRASES) for no-goal sessions instead of a single 'Done.' message
decisions.reason: Prevents the notification from sounding identical every time; maintains the branded prefix while varying the detail to keep the user experience fresh

decisions.decision: Extract shared preconditions (voice_enabled check, macOS gate, say binary lookup) into _say_preconditions() helper
decisions.reason: Eliminates duplication between speak() and speak_signature(); both functions need identical gating logic

decisions.decision: Add voice= parameter to speak() function with empty string default for system default voice
decisions.reason: Allows single-voice calls to specify a particular macOS voice when needed; backward-compatible with existing code that omits the parameter

relational_files.why: stop.py calls load_voice_prefix() and load_voice_body() from config; these loaders must return valid macOS voice names
Contains unit tests for speak(), speak_signature(), and _say_preconditions(); validates the two-voice implementation
Calls speak_signature() from voice.py to announce session completion with the two-voice sonic logo

blockers: load_voice_prefix() and load_voice_body() config loaders must be implemented in askr/state/config.py before the two-voice sonic logo can run in production
